methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $365M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Materials (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Materials, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Noveon Magnetics is a Series C-stage materials company founded by Scott Dunn and Peter Afiuny that has raised $365M to date. The Materials sector peer universe is sparse; while two private comparables exist (Vulcan Elements and Lyten), neither provides sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage data to support a peer-median valuation method. No public Materials companies in the relevant tier were identified for direct comparison.

Given data constraints, stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total capital raised ($365M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical Series C to next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.7x yields an implied valuation of $620M. This single-method triangulation generates a median point estimate of $620M, with a sensitivity range of $527M (low) to $714M (high) reflecting ±15% variance bounds around the central calculation.

The narrow peer universe represents the material constraint in this analysis. The $620M median estimate rests entirely on stage-progression mechanics rather than market-comparable validation. Key uncertainties include whether the 1.7x multiplier accurately reflects Noveon's risk profile and growth trajectory relative to unobserved peer financials, and whether the raised-capital proxy adequately captures current business fundamentals. Additional diligence on recent revenue, unit economics, and IP valuation would be required to validate or adjust this range.
